signals.idea: Multi-select timeline clips via marquee box selection and ctrl+click. Enable batch operations: drag multiple clips, delete multiple, bulk update settings. Draw a selection rectangle over timeline clips to select them, ctrl+click to add/remove from selection. Selected clips should highlight visually. Batch operations: move/drag all selected, delete all selected, bulk edit shared settings in a panel.

unresolved_flags.concern: Timeline selection state: moving `selectedClipIds` into a new `useMultiSelect` hook at `useTimelineState` level breaks the existing selection/data commit lifecycle, so saves, reloads, splits, and external data refreshes can leave stale selections unless the multi-select state is integrated with the save/data layer rather than bolted on afterward.

Clip drag interactions: Step 3's Ctrl/Cmd-click toggle in `ClipAction` is not sufficient because `useClipDrag` already captures pointer events at the document level and unconditionally re-selects the initiating clip on pointer-up, which will fight multi-select toggles and marquee-driven selection semantics.

Timeline drop targeting: the plan's 'same-track only' multi-drag with a rejection indicator is not implementable by changing `useClipDrag.ts` alone, because the current drop-position pipeline never produces a rejected target and silently remaps incompatible rows or creates a new track.

Timeline overlap resolution: Step 9 underestimates same-track multi-drag because the repository's placement logic only resolves overlap for one moved clip, not a batch, so dragging several selected clips into occupied time ranges has no defined collision policy.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 7.5. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 4.

items: Ctrl+Click (Cmd+Click on Mac) toggles individual clips in/out of a multi-selection set
Drawing a marquee rectangle on empty timeline area selects all clips whose bounds intersect the rectangle
All selected clips show a distinct visual highlight (primary clip vs secondary selected)
Pressing Delete/Backspace removes ALL selected clips in one operation
Dragging any clip in a multi-selection moves ALL selected clips together, maintaining their relative time positions
PropertiesPanel shows batch summary when >1 clip selected, with editable shared fields (opacity, speed, mute)
Single-click on a clip still replaces the entire selection (existing behavior preserved)
Context menu on a selected clip shows batch operations (Delete N clips, etc.)
No regressions in single-clip workflows: drag, resize, split, properties editing all work as before

unresolved_flags.concern: Timeline selection state: moving `selectedClipIds` into a new `useMultiSelect` hook at `useTimelineState` level breaks the existing selection/data commit lifecycle, so saves, reloads, splits, and external data refreshes can leave stale selections unless the multi-select state is integrated with the save/data layer rather than bolted on afterward.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineSave.ts:175-205` currently normalizes selection inside `commitData`, including pruning missing clip ids on every data commit. The plan says selection state lives in a new hook under `useTimelineState`, and only mentions cleanup later in Step 14, which misses the fact that the repo already relies on the save layer to keep selection valid during normal edits and reloads.

unresolved_flags.concern: Clip drag interactions: Step 3's Ctrl/Cmd-click toggle in `ClipAction` is not sufficient because `useClipDrag` already captures pointer events at the document level and unconditionally re-selects the initiating clip on pointer-up, which will fight multi-select toggles and marquee-driven selection semantics.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts:144-156` starts a drag session from a document-capture `pointerdown` on `.clip-action`, and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipDrag.ts:244-274` always calls `setSelectedClipId(session.clipId)` / `setSelectedTrackId(...)` on pointer-up even when no drag occurred. Following the plan literally by only changing `ClipAction.tsx` would leave click selection behavior owned by two conflicting paths.

unresolved_flags.concern: Timeline drop targeting: the plan's 'same-track only' multi-drag with a rejection indicator is not implementable by changing `useClipDrag.ts` alone, because the current drop-position pipeline never produces a rejected target and silently remaps incompatible rows or creates a new track.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/lib/drop-position.ts:114-178` always returns `isReject: false`; on kind mismatch it redirects to the nearest compatible track or marks the drop as `isNewTrack`. The plan says multi-track/cross-track batch drag should show rejection, but it does not include corresponding changes to `drop-position.ts` / `useDragCoordinator.ts`, so the proposed behavior cannot happen as written.

unresolved_flags.concern: Timeline overlap resolution: Step 9 underestimates same-track multi-drag because the repository's placement logic only resolves overlap for one moved clip, not a batch, so dragging several selected clips into occupied time ranges has no defined collision policy.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.ts:39-91` moves one clip and then calls `resolveOverlaps(...)` for a single `clipId`; `src/tools/video-editor/lib/resolve-overlaps.ts:75-146` is explicitly written around one 'moved clip' that yields to immovable siblings. The plan says to apply one delta to all selected clips, but does not specify or schedule the group-overlap algorithm needed to preserve relative spacing without producing invalid placements.
